raw_response:
From the given articles, we find consistent emphasis on how the Gleason score determines the histopathological classification of prostate cancer, along with details regarding various types of prostate cancer including their incidence locations and aggressiveness. Article [article-38782_3] mentions that although prostate cancer arises predominantly in the peripheral zone due to its anatomical properties, it highlights that the central zone is relatively spared. Therefore:
\boxed{B} Based on the detailed description in one of the referenced articles ([article-38782_3]), stating that "[t]he zonal anatomy of the prostate was described by McNeal in 1968 and remains in use today, with around 70% to 80% of cancers developing in the peripheral zone and cancer seldom found in the central zone," option B correctly identifies that while peripheral zone tumors are prevalent, the central zone is rarely affected by prostate cancer. Thus, the correct answer is \(\boxed{\text{B}}\).
